title: Control-M Workload Policy Violation and SLA Breach Risk
app: Control-M/EM alerting integration to Splunk HEC
spl:
index=batch (sourcetype="controlm:alert" OR sourcetype="controlm:job")
| eval policy_hit=if(sourcetype="controlm:alert" AND match(lower(alert_type),"(?i)workload|policy|resource"),1,0)
| eval sla_risk=if(sourcetype="controlm:job" AND (sla_met="false" OR match(lower(status),"(?i)late|overdue")),1,0)
| where policy_hit=1 OR sla_risk=1
| stats count, latest(_time) as last_seen, values(job_name) as jobs by policy_name, folder, controlm_server
| sort -count
description: Workload policies enforce CPU, job concurrency, and time-window guardrails; violations predict contention that will breach job SLAs or overrun change windows. Correlating policy alerts with concrete job outcomes separates noisy tuning from real risk.
value: Supports operational risk dashboards and prevents cross-application stampedes on shared mainframe or open-systems capacity.
implementation: Map vendor-specific alert_type strings via lookup. Require `controlm_server` for multi-EM estates. Exclude test folders with an allowlist. Schedule near-real-time for policy alerts, hourly rollups for SLA trending.
visualization: Stacked bar (violations by policy), timeline with annotation markers, pie of affected folders.
grandmaExplanation: We watch batch and control jobs so missed handoffs or resource limits do not break business processing.
knownFalsePositives: Transient errors during platform maintenance, network blips, or certificate renewals on shared services.
